sentence.en: officially state in court what punishment a criminal will receive

ex.en: The judge sentenced him to five years in prison.

sentence.en: a group of words that expresses a complete thought, usually ending with a period

ex.en: Write a sentence using this word.
ex.ko: 이 단어를 사용해서 문장을 만들어 보세요.

sentence.en: the punishment, especially a period in prison, given by a court

ex.en: He is serving a ten-year sentence.

september.en: the ninth month of the year

ex.en: School starts in September.
She has seven pencils in her bag.
ex.ko: 그녀는 가방에 연필 일곱 자루를 가지고 있다.

ex.en: He turned seventeen last week.
My grandmother is seventy years old.

shake.en: move something quickly from side to side or up and down

ex.en: Shake the bottle before you open it.

shake.en: a cold drink made by mixing milk with ice cream or fruit

ex.en: He ordered a chocolate shake.

share.en: use, have, or give something together with other people

ex.en: She shared her lunch with a friend.

share.en: a part of something that belongs to or is given to one person

ex.en: Everyone paid their share of the bill.
ex.ko: 모두가 각자의 몫만큼 계산을 나눠 냈다.

she.en: used to refer to a woman or girl already mentioned

ex.en: She is my sister.

sheep.en: a farm animal with thick woolly fur, kept for its wool or meat

ex.en: The sheep were eating grass in the field.

shelf.en: a flat board fixed to a wall or in a cupboard, used for holding things

ex.en: He put the books back on the shelf.
ex.ko: 그는 책을 다시 선반에 꽂아 두었다.

ship.en: send goods to another place, especially using a delivery service

ex.en: The company ships orders within two days.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 이틀 안에 주문품을 배송한다.

ship.en: a large boat that carries people or goods on water

ex.en: The ship sailed across the ocean.

shirt.en: a piece of clothing worn on the upper body, often with a collar and buttons

ex.en: He wore a blue shirt to the interview.
ex.ko: 그는 면접에 파란 셔츠를 입고 갔다.

shoe.en: a covering worn on the foot, usually made of leather or other strong material

ex.en: She bought a new pair of shoes.
ex.ko: 그녀는 새 신발 한 켤레를 샀다.

shop.en: go to a store to buy things

ex.en: We went shopping for new shoes.
ex.ko: 우리는 새 신발을 사러 쇼핑하러 갔다.

shop.en: a small store where goods are sold

ex.en: She works at a flower shop.

shopping.en: the activity of going to stores to buy things

ex.en: I hate shopping on weekends.

short.en: not long in length, distance, height, or time

ex.en: He is short for his age.

should.en: used to say what is the right or sensible thing to do

ex.en: You should see a doctor.

shoulder.en: the part of the body between the neck and the arm

ex.en: She carried the bag on her shoulder.

show.en: a form of entertainment such as a television program or a performance

ex.en: We watched a comedy show last night.
Can you show me your ticket?

shower.en: give someone a large amount of something generously

ex.en: They showered him with praise.

shower.en: an act of washing your body under a spray of water, or the device used for this

ex.en: I take a shower every morning.
A sudden shower caught us on the way home.
ex.ko: 갑작스러운 소나기가 집에 가는 길에 우리를 덮쳤다.

shy.en: nervous or uncomfortable around other people, especially people you don't know

ex.en: The little boy was too shy to say hello.
ex.ko: 그 어린 남자아이는 너무 수줍어서 인사를 못 했다.

sick.en: suffering from an illness or feeling unwell

ex.en: She stayed home because she was sick.

sick.en: annoyed or bored because you have had too much of something

ex.en: I'm sick of this rainy weather.
ex.ko: 나는 이 비 오는 날씨가 지긋지긋하다.

side.en: a position to the left or right of something, or one surface of an object

ex.en: He stood on the other side of the room.

side.en: one of two or more groups that are opposed in a contest or argument

ex.en: Which side are you on?
Her sight is not as good as it used to be.

sight.en: something interesting or beautiful that you see, especially somewhere you are visiting

ex.en: The Grand Canyon is an amazing sight.

sign.en: write your name on a document to show you agree with it

ex.en: Please sign your name here.

sign.en: a board or piece of paper that gives information or instructions

ex.en: Follow the signs to the exit.

sign.en: something that shows a fact, quality, or feeling exists

ex.en: Dark clouds are a sign of rain.

sing.en: make musical sounds with your voice

ex.en: The children sang a birthday song.

singer.en: a person who sings, especially as a job

ex.en: She wants to be a famous singer.
ex.ko: 그녀는 유명한 가수가 되고 싶어 한다.

sir.en: a polite way of speaking to a man, especially one you do not know

ex.en: Excuse me, sir, is this seat taken?
ex.ko: 실례합니다, 선생님. 이 자리 사람 있나요?

sister.en: a girl or woman who has the same parents as you

ex.en: I have two sisters.

sit.en: rest your weight on your bottom, with your back straight

ex.en: Please sit down and relax.

site.en: a place where something is located or is going to be built

ex.en: They are building a new school on this site.
ex.ko: 그들은 이 부지에 새 학교를 짓고 있다.

site.en: a place on the internet where you can find information about a particular subject

ex.en: This site has useful information.
There are six chairs around the table.
ex.ko: 탁자 주위에 의자가 여섯 개 있다.

ex.en: She just turned sixteen.
ex.ko: 그녀는 이제 막 열여섯 살이 되었다.

ex.en: My uncle will turn sixty next year.
ex.ko: 내 삼촌은 내년에 예순 살이 된다.

size.en: how big or small something is

ex.en: What size shoes do you wear?

skill.en: the ability to do something well, usually gained through training or practice

ex.en: Cooking is a useful skill.

skirt.en: a piece of clothing worn by women and girls that hangs from the waist

ex.en: She wore a blue skirt to school.
ex.ko: 그녀는 학교에 파란 치마를 입고 갔다.

sky.en: the space above the earth where the sun, moon, and clouds are

ex.en: The sky is very blue today.

sleep.en: the natural rest your body and mind take when your eyes are closed and you are not conscious

ex.en: I need eight hours of sleep.

sleep.en: rest with your eyes closed and your mind and body not active

ex.en: The baby is sleeping in her room.

slow.en: not fast; taking a long time to move or happen

ex.en: The train was very slow today.

slow.en: make something go at a lower speed

ex.en: He slowed the car as he reached the corner.
ex.ko: 그는 모퉁이에 다다르자 차의 속도를 줄였다.

small.en: not large in size or amount

ex.en: They live in a small apartment.
She is a smart student.
He looked smart in his new suit.
ex.ko: 그는 새 정장을 입어 단정해 보였다.

smell.en: notice an odor with your nose, or give off a particular odor

ex.en: This soup smells delicious.

smell.en: the quality that you notice through your nose

ex.en: There is a strange smell in the kitchen.

smile.en: move the corners of your mouth upward to show happiness or friendliness

ex.en: She smiled when she saw her friend.

smile.en: a happy expression made by turning up the corners of your mouth

ex.en: He greeted us with a warm smile.

smith.en: a person who makes things out of metal by hammering it while it is hot

ex.en: The village smith made horseshoes.

smoke.en: breathe in and out the smoke from a burning cigarette

ex.en: He went outside to smoke.

smoke.en: the gray or black cloud produced when something burns

ex.en: Smoke filled the room.
Smoking is not allowed here.

snake.en: a long, thin reptile with no legs that moves by sliding along the ground

ex.en: A snake crossed the path in front of us.
ex.ko: 뱀 한 마리가 우리 앞의 길을 가로질렀다.

ex.en: It snowed all night.

snow.en: soft white pieces of frozen water that fall from the sky in cold weather

ex.en: The children were playing in the snow.
We had a snowy winter this year.
ex.ko: 올해 우리는 눈이 많이 오는 겨울을 보냈다.

so.en: used to introduce the result of something just mentioned

ex.en: It was raining, so we stayed home.
I'm so happy to see you.

soccer.cue: 발로 공을 차서 골을 넣는 운동
soccer.en: a game in which two teams try to kick a ball into the other team's goal

ex.en: He plays soccer every weekend.

social.en: relating to society, or relating to friendly activities with other people

ex.en: She has a busy social life.

sofa.en: a long, comfortable seat with a back and arms, for two or more people

ex.en: He fell asleep on the sofa.

solve.en: find the answer to a problem or question

ex.en: Can you solve this math problem?
ex.ko: 이 수학 문제를 풀 수 있니?

some.en: an amount or number of something that is not stated exactly

ex.en: I need some water.

someone.en: a person who is not known or named

ex.en: Someone left this umbrella here.
ex.ko: 누군가 여기에 이 우산을 두고 갔다.

something.en: a thing that is not known or named

ex.en: I have something to tell you.
They have two sons and a daughter.
ex.ko: 그들은 아들 둘과 딸 하나가 있다.

song.en: a short piece of music with words that you sing

ex.en: She sang her favorite song.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신이 가장 좋아하는 노래를 불렀다.

soon.en: in a short time from now

ex.en: The bus will arrive soon.

sorry.en: feeling sad or regretful about something you did or something bad that happened

ex.en: I'm sorry I'm late.

soup.en: a liquid food made by cooking vegetables, meat, or fish in water

ex.en: She made chicken soup for dinner.

speak.en: talk to someone using your voice

ex.en: Can you speak more slowly?

special.en: different from what is usual, and often better

ex.en: This is a special day for us.

speech.en: a formal talk given to a group of people

ex.en: The president gave a speech about the economy.

spell.en: say or write the letters of a word in the correct order

ex.en: Can you spell your name for me?

spell.en: words that are believed to have magical power

ex.en: The witch cast a spell on the prince.

spend.en: pay money for something, or use a period of time doing something

ex.en: We spent the weekend at the beach.

sport.en: a physical activity in which people compete against each other

ex.en: Swimming is my favorite sport.

spot.en: notice or see something, especially something that is hard to notice

ex.en: I spotted my friend in the crowd.

spot.en: a small round mark on a surface

ex.en: The dog has black spots on its fur.
ex.ko: 그 개는 털에 검은 점들이 있다.

ex.en: This is a great spot for a picnic.

spy.en: a person whose job is to secretly find out information about another country or organization

ex.en: The movie is about a spy during the Cold War.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 냉전 시대의 한 스파이에 관한 이야기이다.

stage.en: a point or period in a process or in the development of something

ex.en: The project is still in an early stage.

stage.en: a raised platform where performers stand in a theater

ex.en: The singer walked onto the stage.

stand.en: be on your feet in an upright position

ex.en: Please stand up when your name is called.

stand.en: be able to accept or deal with something unpleasant

ex.en: I can't stand this noise.
ex.ko: 나는 이 소음을 참을 수가 없다.

stand.en: a small structure or table where things are sold or displayed

ex.en: She bought lemonade at a stand.

star.en: have the most important role in a film, play, or show

ex.en: The movie stars a young actress.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 젊은 여배우가 주연을 맡았다.

star.en: a bright object in the night sky

ex.en: You can see many stars tonight.
ex.ko: 오늘 밤에는 별을 많이 볼 수 있다.

star.en: a famous and successful performer, such as an actor or singer

ex.en: He became a movie star.

start.en: the point at which something begins

ex.en: The start of the race was delayed by rain.
Let's start the meeting.

station.en: a building or place where trains or buses stop for passengers

ex.en: I'll meet you at the train station.

stay.en: a period of time spent in a place

ex.en: We enjoyed our stay in Seoul.

stay.en: continue to be in a place without leaving

ex.en: Please stay here until I come back.
ex.ko: 내가 돌아올 때까지 여기 머물러 있어 주세요.

step.en: the action of putting one foot in front of the other while walking

ex.en: She took a step back.

step.en: one of a series of actions taken to achieve something

ex.en: The first step is to make a plan.
ex.ko: 첫 번째 단계는 계획을 세우는 것이다.

step.en: move by putting one foot in front of the other

ex.en: She stepped onto the stage.

still.en: continuing to happen or be true up to now

ex.en: He still lives with his parents.
It was raining, but we still went out.
Please stay still.
He threw a stone into the lake.
The car stopped at the light.

stop.en: a place where a bus or train regularly stops for passengers

ex.en: Get off at the next stop.
We store extra food in the basement.

store.en: a place where you can buy things (American English)

ex.en: I bought milk at the store.

story.en: an account of real or imaginary events told to entertain people

ex.en: She told us a funny story.
ex.ko: 그녀는 우리에게 재미있는 이야기를 해 주었다.

story.en: one level of a building (American English)

ex.en: They live in a three-story house.
Draw a straight line.

straight.en: in a direct line, without turning

ex.en: Go straight ahead.

strange.en: unusual or unexpected in a way that surprises or confuses you

ex.en: I heard a strange noise last night.

street.en: a public road in a town or city, usually with buildings on both sides

ex.en: He lives on a quiet street.
Our teacher is very strict about homework.
ex.ko: 우리 선생님은 숙제에 대해 아주 엄격하시다.

strong.en: having a lot of physical power or force

ex.en: He is strong enough to lift that box.
ex.ko: 그는 저 상자를 들 만큼 힘이 세다.

student.en: a person who is studying at a school, college, or university

ex.en: She is a university student.

study.en: detailed research into a subject to discover new facts

ex.en: He is doing a study on climate change.
ex.ko: 그는 기후 변화에 관한 연구를 하고 있다.

study.en: spend time learning about a subject

ex.en: She studies English every evening.

subject.en: a topic that is discussed, written about, or studied at school

ex.en: Math is my favorite subject.

subway.en: an underground train system in a city

ex.en: I take the subway to work.

successful.en: achieving the result that you wanted

ex.en: The concert was very successful.

sugar.en: a sweet substance used to make food and drinks taste sweeter

ex.en: Do you take sugar in your coffee?

suggestion.en: an idea or plan that you offer to someone

ex.en: Do you have any suggestions for dinner?

sum.en: the total amount you get by adding numbers together, or an amount of money

ex.en: The sum of five and three is eight.

summer.cue: 일 년 중 가장 더운 계절
summer.en: the warmest season of the year, between spring and fall

ex.en: We go swimming every summer.

sun.en: the star that gives light and heat to the earth

ex.en: The sun rises in the east.

sunday.en: the day of the week between Saturday and Monday

ex.en: We go to church on Sundays.

sunny.en: having a lot of bright sunlight

ex.en: It's a sunny day today.

sunshine.en: the light and warmth that comes from the sun

ex.en: The children played in the sunshine.
That's a super idea!

supermarket.en: a large store that sells food and other household goods

ex.en: I need to go to the supermarket.

sure.en: having no doubt that something is true

ex.en: Are you sure about that?

sure.en: used to say yes or to agree to something

ex.en: "Can you help me?" "Sure!"

surf.en: ride on ocean waves while standing on a special board

ex.en: He learned to surf in Hawaii.

surf.en: look at different websites on the internet without a particular purpose

ex.en: He likes to surf the internet at night.
ex.ko: 그는 밤에 인터넷을 돌아다니는 것을 좋아한다.

surprise.cue: 예상 못 한 일로 놀라게 하다
surprise.en: cause someone to feel astonished because of something unexpected

ex.en: Her answer surprised everyone.

surprise.en: a feeling of astonishment caused by something unexpected, or the unexpected event itself

ex.en: What a nice surprise!

survey.en: a set of questions asked to many people to find out their opinions

ex.en: We did a survey of student opinions.
This cake is very sweet.

sweet.en: a small piece of candy (British English)

ex.en: The children love sweets.

swim.en: move through water using your arms and legs

ex.en: They swim in the lake every summer.
ex.ko: 그들은 매년 여름 그 호수에서 수영한다.

swimming.en: the sport or activity of moving through water using your arms and legs

ex.en: Swimming is good exercise.

swimming pool.en: a large container of water built for people to swim in

ex.en: The hotel has an outdoor swimming pool.

t-shirt.en: a simple shirt with short sleeves and no collar

ex.en: He wore a white T-shirt and jeans.

table.en: a piece of furniture with a flat top and legs, used for eating or working on

ex.en: They sat around the table for dinner.

table.en: information arranged in rows and columns

ex.en: The results are shown in the table below.

talk.en: a conversation between two or more people

ex.en: We had a long talk about the future.
ex.ko: 우리는 미래에 대해 긴 대화를 나눴다.

talk.en: say things to someone using your voice

ex.en: Can I talk to you for a minute?
ex.ko: 잠깐 얘기 좀 할 수 있을까?

tall.en: having a greater than average height

ex.en: My brother is very tall.

taxi.en: a car with a driver that you pay to take you somewhere

ex.en: We took a taxi to the airport.

tea.en: a hot drink made by pouring boiling water over the dried leaves of the tea plant

ex.en: Would you like a cup of tea?

teach.en: help someone learn something by giving them information or showing them how to do it

ex.en: She teaches math at the school.

teacher.en: a person whose job is to teach students

ex.en: My teacher is very kind.

team.en: a group of people who work or play together

ex.en: Our team won the game.

technology.en: the use of scientific knowledge for practical purposes, especially in machines and equipment

ex.en: New technology has changed the way we live.
ex.ko: 새로운 기술이 우리가 사는 방식을 바꾸어 놓았다.

ex.en: She telephoned her mother to say hello.

telephone.en: a device used to talk to someone in another place

ex.en: The telephone rang three times.

television.en: a device that receives broadcast signals and shows moving pictures with sound

ex.en: They watched television all evening.

tell.en: give information to someone by speaking or writing

ex.en: Tell me what happened.

temple.en: a building used for worship, especially in some religions other than Christianity

ex.en: They visited an ancient temple in Kyoto.

temple.en: the flat area on either side of your forehead

ex.en: He felt a pain in his temple.
She counted from one to ten.

tennis.en: a game in which players use rackets to hit a ball over a net

ex.en: They play tennis every Saturday.
The weather was terrible yesterday.

test.en: try something to find out how good, accurate, or reliable it is

ex.en: They tested the new engine.

test.en: a set of questions used to measure someone's knowledge or ability

ex.en: I have a math test tomorrow.

than.en: used to compare two things or amounts

ex.en: She is taller than her brother.

thank.en: tell someone that you are grateful for something

ex.en: I want to thank you for your help.

thanks.en: words used to express that you are grateful

ex.en: Thanks for the gift!

that.en: to the degree stated or implied, especially in negative sentences

ex.en: I'm not that hungry.

that.cue: 멀리 있거나 이미 언급된 것을 가리켜
that.en: used to refer to a specific thing, person, or idea that is not close or already mentioned

ex.en: That is my house.

the.cue: 이미 알고 있는 특정한 것을 가리켜
the.en: used before a noun to refer to a specific thing that is already known

ex.en: Close the door, please.

theater.en: a building where plays or movies are performed or shown

ex.en: We watched a play at the theater.

their.en: belonging to the people or things already mentioned

ex.en: That is their house.

them.en: used to refer to people or things already mentioned, as the object of a verb or preposition

ex.en: I saw them at the park.
I was still in school then.
We had dinner, and then we watched a movie.
ex.ko: 우리는 저녁을 먹고, 그러고 나서 영화를 봤다.

ex.en: Put the box over there.

there.en: used to say that something exists or happens

ex.en: There is a cat on the roof.

these.en: used to refer to people or things that are near or already mentioned

ex.en: These are my books.

they.en: used to refer to people, animals, or things already mentioned

ex.en: They live next door.

thick.en: having a large distance between the two opposite surfaces of something

ex.en: She wore a thick coat in winter.

thin.en: having a small distance between opposite surfaces, or having little body fat

ex.en: He cut the bread into thin slices.

thing.en: an object, event, or idea that is not specifically named

ex.en: I have a lot of things to do today.

think.en: use your mind to consider something, or have an opinion about it

ex.en: I think it will rain tomorrow.
ex.ko: 나는 내일 비가 올 거라고 생각해.

ex.en: She will be thirteen next month.
ex.ko: 그녀는 다음 달에 열세 살이 된다.

ex.en: He turned thirty last year.

this.en: used to refer to a person or thing that is near or being talked about now

ex.en: This is my new phone.

those.en: used to refer to people or things that are farther away or already mentioned

ex.en: Those are my parents over there.
She has three brothers.
